channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: 'The thing that's amazing to me is this was 3D-printed': Pentagon's SciTech supremo describes future of warfare where projectiles are made up of coconut husks and coffee grounds
item.description: The Ukraine war has fundamentally changed the Pentagon's science chief's understanding of the role of AI and biotechnology in future conflicts

item.title: Of course Samantha Morton will be the best part of The Odyssey — this 'devastating' drama you can stream now proved why long before Christopher Nolan's standing ovation
item.description: Samantha Morton's performance as Circe in The Odyssey has the Christopher Nolan seal of approval — but there's an obvious reason why we should have seen her brilliance coming.

item.title: Google’s deepfake detector system used to debunk McConnell hoax pic
item.description: Earlier this week, a picture seemed to show Kentucky Senator Mitch McConnell covered in tubes in a hospital bed in a state of extreme distress. It turned out to be an AI-generated fake.

item.title: With EU backing, QuantumDiamonds aims to speed up chip manufacturing
item.description: Like its U.S. counterpart, the European Chips Act aims to foster the semiconductor industry — in part thanks to state subsidies. One of the beneficiaries is QuantumDiamonds, a German startup that applies a novel approach to inspecting chips.

item.title: Grok 4.5 par xAI : l’IA d’Elon Musk pour coder à prix cassé fait l’impasse sur l’Europe
item.description: SpaceXAI lance Grok 4.5, son premier modèle pensé d’abord pour le code. Une info qui compte : l’Europe devra patienter jusqu’à mi-juillet pour y toucher.

item.title: The FTC Settlement With John Deere Is a Huge Win for the Right-to-Repair Movement
item.description: After more than a decade of pushback, farmers and repair advocates have won access to equipment and services John Deere had long kept under its control.

item.title: The 'world's most powerful AI data center' hit with massive class-action lawsuit — Wisconsin residents allege noise and extreme light pollution at $7.3 billion Microsoft mega-facility
item.description: Microsoft is hit with a lawsuit from residents alleging noise, light, and dust pollution at its new AI data center.

item.title: Microsoft reportedly cancels Avowed sequel as Obsidian focuses on a Fallout game
item.description: The Fallout: New Vegas developer has not escaped Microsoft's Xbox layoffs unscathed.

item.title: I Built a Self-Improving AI, and So Can You
item.description: Experiments in using AI to build AI show that the future doesn’t just belong to the frontier labs.

item.title: Autonomous drone delivery startup Manna plots major US expansion
item.description: Manna is launching a U.S. operations and manufacturing facility in Tulsa, Oklahoma, that will eventually employ 1,000 people.

item.title: FTC reaches settlement that brings right-to-repair to John Deere farm equipment
item.description: The FTC sued the famed green tractor company last year.

item.title: Get a $30 credit when you reserve Samsung’s upcoming Galaxy phones
item.description: Even though they haven’t been officially announced yet, Samsung is giving you a chance to save some cash when you preorder what we’re expecting to be the brand’s updated Galaxy Z Fold phones. The next Galaxy Unpacked event will take place on July 22nd, 2026, and features the tagline “A new shape unfolds.” In addition […]

item.title: ‘Lucky that we didn’t listen to the internet’ — Black Ops 7 isn’t perfect, but I’ll be sad to leave its incredible multiplayer behind for Modern Warfare 4
item.description: Despite a rocky launch, Black Ops 7 has delivered the best multiplayer Call of Duty experience in years.

item.title: Peter Thiel’s Husband Sued a Flight Attendant Who Says He Assaulted Her on a Private Jet
item.description: Matthew Danzeisen’s lawyer says the case is a “shakedown about a bag” that brushed someone’s leg. Stefanie Bojar says she was injured aboard the jet—and that the lawsuit is a bullying tactic.

item.title: The Odyssey sails into rave reviews and online backlash
item.description: 'The Odyssey' first reactions praise Christopher Nolan’s IMAX epic as casting backlash continues online.

item.title: Multiple US Army websites defaced by hackers critical of Trump — error page displays message criticizing President
item.description: The websites were defaced with pro-Kurdish media, and messages directed at Trump and Tom Barrack.

item.title: Erling Haaland is somehow a Love and Deepspace star now
item.description: AI-generated football memes are taking over the 2026 World Cup, with Haaland, Mbappé, Ronaldo, Messi, and more becoming fan-edit material

item.title: Meta Ray-Ban glasses brick the camera if you mess with recording light
item.description: Meta announced new mandatory safety update to its smart glasses, preventing users from tampering with recording light.

item.title: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an ‘Opus-class model’
item.description: Elon Musk's tech company released the newest version of Grok on Wednesday, promising a cheaper, more efficient alternative to other powerful AI models.

item.title: ‘Slow-cial’ app Roost forces you to slow down to the speed of a carrier pigeon
item.description: This developer didn't expect his side project to grow to 300,000 users, but people love Roost because it's an alternative to an always-on, fast-paced online culture.

item.title: This startup thinks robotics is about to have its ChatGPT moment
item.description: General Intuition is betting millions of hours of video game data can train the foundation models for physical AI, making it easier to build smarter robots with minimal real-world data.
